description_long: This marble sculpture depicts a standing male figure draped in a classical robe, with his right hand resting on his hip and his left arm holding a staff entwined by a serpent, reminiscent of the Greek god Asclepius. The figure's head is adorned with a headband or fillet, and his facial features are partially obscured by damage or weathering. The robe is intricately carved, with deep folds cascading down the body and pooling at the feet, which are firmly planted on a rectangular plinth. The back of the sculpture shows the robe gathered and draped over the left shoulder, with the right arm and back exposed. The surface of the sculpture is worn, suggesting age and exposure to the elements, and the coloration is a mottled off-white with subtle grayish tones.
description: A classical marble statue of a robed male figure holding a staff with a serpent, standing on a rectangular plinth. The sculpture features detailed drapery and a weathered, off-white surface.
description_view_0: Front view showing the figure's face, right hand on hip, and left hand holding a staff with a serpent.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the depth of the drapery and the full length of the staff and serpent.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the robe's draping over the back and the exposed right arm and back.
description_view_3: Right side view focusing on the figure's posture and the way the robe wraps around the body.
attribute_subject_matter: classical human figure with staff and serpent
attribute_location_context: The sculpture is displayed on a simple rectangular plinth, suggesting an outdoor or indoor public setting such as a park, plaza, or museum.
